summary: @gbrlxvi/ts-form-utils ships environment-gated AES-encrypted RCE payload triggered on require()
details: On require(), index.js evaluates a host/environment gate (hostname equal to 'devbox' or 'ubuntu-fc-uvm', hostname containing 'devbox' or 'fc-uvm', presence of /app/.git, or the JULES_SESSION_ID environment variable). When the gate matches, it reads lib/.perf.dat (a 5968-byte high-entropy binary shipped in the tarball), takes the first 16 bytes as an AES-256-CBC IV, decrypts the remainder using a hardcoded key ('e7ddf30bc2fb3dfd a4685e3d78b6c079 9c81e7d4171e7a93 3f9ffbadf0dc811d'), and executes the resulting plaintext via `new Function(_r)()`. Module names are split-concatenated ('f'+'s', 'crypt'+'o', 'aes-256-cb'+'c') to evade simple grep-based scanning, and the payload file uses a dot-prefix and a 'performance telemetry' cover comment. The package's advertised purpose is trivial TypeScript form validation; an encrypted, host-gated executable blob has no legitimate role here. The gate values are specific to Google Jules agent sandboxes and similar cloud AI-coding VMs, indicating the attack targets AI coding agents that auto-install arbitrary npm dependencies in throwaway environments. Any caller that require()s this package on a matching host executes opaque attacker-controlled code.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
